mainEntity.name: I fell in the parking lot walking into work in Salt Lake—does that even count?

acceptedAnswer.text: Usually, yes. If the parking lot is owned or controlled by your employer, falls occurring there while arriving at or leaving work are typically covered under the 'premises rule.'
acceptedAnswer.description: If you fell in a parking lot, your eligibility for workers' comp depends on the "Premises Rule." Usually, if the parking lot is owned, maintained, or provided by the employer, the injury is covered. If it’s a public lot or street, it might not be. In Salt Lake City, many office buildings have shared garages; if your employer requires you to park there or it is the only way to access your job, you are likely covered. These cases are highly technical and often end up in front of the Labor Commission. You should consult an attorney to determine if the specific location of your fall qualifies as being "at work."
